prompt:
Goal: Create a high-fashion streetwear campaign poster for a limited-edition drop, centered on a full-body model wearing an oversized black graphic hoodie and black sweatpants with hot-pink graffiti branding.

Canvas: Vertical portrait poster, approximately 2:3 aspect ratio, off-white studio background with subtle paper grain, slight shadows on the floor and wall, editorial fashion lighting.

Layout: Huge condensed hot-pink headline text fills the entire top background, reading HER19845. Place the model in the center foreground, overlapping the headline so the letters appear behind the head and torso. Keep the composition bold, poster-like, and slightly grungy.

Subject details: A young adult streetwear model stands relaxed with legs apart and hands in pockets, long brown hair falling over the shoulders, face softly obscured or blurred, wearing layered silver necklaces, a white graphic T-shirt, an oversized black zip hoodie with white and hot-pink punk graphics, loose black sweatpants with large pink and white graffiti text, and pink-and-white sneakers. The visible outfit has exactly 6 major pieces: black hoodie, white graphic T-shirt, black sweatpants, pink-and-white sneakers, silver necklace stack, and long loose hair.

Text and graphics: Include exactly 7 secondary text/graphic clusters around the model: 1) left mid block reading “LIMITED EDITION / WORLDWIDE DROP” with three small stars and the slogan “SHE’S NOT HERE / TO FIT IN / SHE’S HERE / TO TAKE OVER.” in pink; 2) left vertical icon stack with a globe icon, circled H icon, outlined star icon, tiny abstract symbols, barcode, and small text “HER19845™”; 3) lower-left circular hot-pink globe stamp with surrounding microtext about global drop and rebel/hacker energy; 4) large black handwritten signature across the right side reading Her 19845, underlined with expressive marker strokes; 5) right mid small outlined pink label box containing a globe icon and “19845”; 6) right lower manifesto block reading “BUILT DIFFERENT. / BREAKS RULES. / MAKES HISTORY.” with three stars, then “REBEL. RARE. REAL.” in pink and “THIS ISN’T FASHION. / THIS IS FREEDOM.” beneath it plus a small signature line; 7) bottom-right row of four small symbols: black sparkle star, black globe, black vertical bars, and a pink star inside a black square.

Visual style: Magazine cover meets underground streetwear lookbook, clean studio photography mixed with distressed punk typography, hot pink and black on off-white, sharp high-resolution product detail, slightly worn ink texture on the giant headline, graffiti marks on clothing, premium editorial realism.

Constraints: Use exactly the text clusters and symbols listed, avoid adding extra logos or watermarks, keep the face intentionally non-identifiable, make the poster feel like a collectible fashion drop announcement for HER19845.
